reframe: hedge-matched claims ('traditionally attributed') vs sophia's hedged evidence; frozen NLI vs healthy semantic incumbent
verdict: NO-GO (robust). Hedge-matching the claims HELPED (NLI answerable-coverage 0.06->0.43) but did not fix the over-abstention: NLI admits only 43% of true hedge-matched attributions vs the healthy semantic incumbent's 93% (answerable-coverage drop 0.50 >> 0.01). NLI's tiny F1 edge (real +0.031 CI[0,0.065] underpowered; forge +0.05 CI[0.033,0.093] internal-only) comes from correctly REJECTING wrong-author claims via contradiction, but at the cost of missing over half the true claims — because sophia's TERSE 3-sentence wiki prose often doesn't clearly enough state the attribution for the NLI cross-encoder to entail. This CONFIRMS the retrieval-bound thesis from the other side: entailment needs RICH evidence (FEVER sentences -> GO); on sophia's terse hedged prose it over-abstains (NO-GO), whether claims are blunt (D2) or hedge-matched (D3). sophia-domain qualifier remains OPEN; prerequisites: richer/longer sophia-domain evidence + more labeled data. Standing result unchanged: retrieval-bound GO on FEVER only. canClaimAGI=false.
guardMetricNote: abstention guard uses ANSWERABLE-coverage (fraction of SUPPORTED claims admitted) per the protocol's wording ('refusing ANSWERABLE questions'); an earlier draft used TOTAL coverage, which wrongly penalized NLI for correctly REJECTING refuted claims the over-admitting semantic incumbent accepts. Corrected.

hedgeMatchingEffect.reading: hedge-matching RAISED NLI's answerable-coverage from ~0.06 (blunt claims, D2) to 0.43 — the framing fix helped — but NLI still misses >half the true hedge-matched claims on sophia's TERSE 3-sentence wiki prose, so it still fails the ≤0.01 answerable-coverage guard vs semantic's 0.93.
